subject: UX & Research
title: Haben Girma: Deafblind Advocate
description: A 60-second lesson on Haben Girma: Deafblind Advocate.

hook.text: Haben Girma is the first deafblind graduate of Harvard Law. She uses a braille display to read and type.

buildup.text: Girma advocates for digital accessibility worldwide. She challenged Facebook to improve its platform.

discovery.text: Her memoir, 'Haben,' shows that disability creates unique perspectives that drive better design for all.

twist.text: Girma argues disability drives innovation. Without blind users, screen readers might never have existed.

climax.text: She proves that access isn't charity. It's engineering. Remove barriers and talent emerges everywhere.

punchline.text: Fix barriers, not people. Disability drives the innovation that helps everyone.

quiz.question: What is Haben Girma's main argument about disability and technology?
options: Disability drives innovation — assistive technologies often become mainstream tools for everyone
Technology should focus on curing disabilities
Deafblind users should use separate specialized devices
